styles.source: inferred: Angular Router uses Routes arrays with path/component/children/loadChildren; Routes API at https://angular.dev/api/router/Routes

historyStrategies.source: inferred: HashLocationStrategy opt-in via withHashLocation() or {useHash:true}; https://angular.dev/api/common/HashLocationStrategy

concepts.source: inferred: Routes support literal paths like {path: 'users'}; Angular Router core API
inferred: wildcard ** matches any unmatched URL; https://angular.dev/guide/routing/common-router-tasks
inferred: children property on Route produces nested routes rendered in child router-outlet

examples.note: Dynamic segment; accessed via ActivatedRoute.paramMap or @Input() when withComponentInputBinding() enabled.
Wildcard route; must be declared last in Routes array.
Nested routes require a <router-outlet> inside the parent component.

supported.source: inferred: Angular SSR (formerly Universal) via @angular/ssr; https://angular.dev/guide/ssr

clientDetection.source: inferred: idiomatic SSR guard using @angular/common PLATFORM_ID token; https://angular.dev/api/common/isPlatformBrowser

hooks.name: NavigationStart
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events (Router service injected via Angular DI); filter by instanceof NavigationStart

availableApi.name: event.restoredState
availableApi.description: Popstate state snapshot when navigation triggered by back/forward.

hooks.name: RouteConfigLoadStart
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof RouteConfigLoadStart

availableApi.name: event.route
availableApi.description: The Route config about to be lazy-loaded.

hooks.name: RouteConfigLoadEnd
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof RouteConfigLoadEnd

hooks.name: RoutesRecognized
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof RoutesRecognized

availableApi.name: event.urlAfterRedirects
availableApi.description: Final URL after configured redirects applied.

availableApi.name: event.state
availableApi.description: RouterStateSnapshot with the matched route tree; walk state.root to access ActivatedRouteSnapshot nodes including routeConfig.path, params, data.

hooks.name: GuardsCheckStart
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof GuardsCheckStart

hooks.name: GuardsCheckEnd
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof GuardsCheckEnd

availableApi.name: event.shouldActivate
availableApi.description: Boolean: whether guards permitted activation; false triggers NavigationCancel afterwards.

hooks.name: ResolveStart
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof ResolveStart

hooks.name: ResolveEnd
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof ResolveEnd

availableApi.name: event.state
availableApi.description: RouterStateSnapshot with resolved data on ActivatedRouteSnapshot.data.

hooks.name: ActivationStart
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof ActivationStart

availableApi.name: event.snapshot
availableApi.description: ActivatedRouteSnapshot of the route being activated (one per activation).

hooks.name: ActivationEnd
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof ActivationEnd

hooks.name: ChildActivationStart
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof ChildActivationStart

availableApi.name: event.snapshot
availableApi.description: ActivatedRouteSnapshot of the parent whose children are activating.

hooks.name: ChildActivationEnd
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof ChildActivationEnd

availableApi.name: event.snapshot
availableApi.description: ActivatedRouteSnapshot of the parent whose children activated.

hooks.name: NavigationEnd
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof NavigationEnd (or use router.events.pipe(filter(e => e instanceof NavigationEnd)))

availableApi.name: event.urlAfterRedirects
availableApi.description: Final URL committed to the browser.

availableApi.name: router.routerState.snapshot
availableApi.description: RouterStateSnapshot of the activated route tree; accessible via Router service.

availableApi.name: router.routerState.root
availableApi.description: ActivatedRoute root; traverse firstChild chain to reach leaf; each node exposes routeConfig.path and snapshot.params.

hooks.name: NavigationCancel
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof NavigationCancel

hooks.name: NavigationError
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof NavigationError

hooks.name: NavigationSkipped
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof NavigationSkipped

hooks.name: Scroll
hooks.access: Subscribe to Router.events; filter by instanceof Scroll (only fired when withInMemoryScrolling() feature enabled)

availableApi.name: event.position
availableApi.description: [x, y] scroll restore position, or null.

routingChanges: Deprecated CanLoad in favor of CanMatch guard.
Added withComponentInputBinding() feature to bind route params/data/queryParams as component @Input.
redirectTo may be a function receiving ActivatedRouteSnapshot.
Router events surface NavigationSkipped with refined NavigationSkippedCode enum.
withNavigationErrorHandler() router feature added for structured error handling.
Standalone APIs by default (affects provideRouter usage patterns).
Event replay hydration integrated with router for SSR.
Further tightening of typed route data/params; continued stabilization of signal-based inputs with router input binding.
entries.source: inferred: Angular v20 release notes; date approximate from Angular's 6-month cadence

structured_output.notes:
Documentation could not be fetched (WebFetch permission denied); all fields were populated from training knowledge of Angular Router and marked 'inferred'. Stage 2 should independently verify any API names (especially event property shapes and NavigationCancellationCode/NavigationSkippedCode enum members) against live angular.dev docs before generating code.

Key Angular-specific nuances for downstream stages:
- Router.events emits *all* event types on one Observable; SDK must filter by instanceof.
- Route tree reconstruction: after NavigationEnd, walk router.routerState.root.firstChild recursively; each ActivatedRouteSnapshot has routeConfig?.path (literal pattern, may be '', ':id', '**') and params. Concatenate non-empty path segments to build the view name.
- Lazy-loaded routes: RouteConfigLoadEnd fires before RoutesRecognized; no special handling usually needed for view-name extraction since final state is captured at NavigationEnd.
- Guard-triggered redirect (returning UrlTree): fires NavigationCancel with code=Redirect, then a new NavigationStart for the redirect target — SDK should treat only the final NavigationEnd as the committed view.
- Config redirects ({path:'a', redirectTo:'b'}) are resolved in-flight and surfaced via urlAfterRedirects on RoutesRecognized/NavigationEnd — no cancel/restart.
- Angular does not have a first-class 'optional segment' syntax; optional data is typically expressed via matrix params or multiple Route entries.
- withHashLocation() is the modern way to enable hash strategy (replaces {useHash:true}).
- SSR via @angular/ssr: the Router runs server-side; use isPlatformBrowser(PLATFORM_ID) before touching window/document in any SDK subscriber.
